context.project_description: Implement LLM token usage tracking to show how many tokens each query costs by integrating with the existing instrumentation system and adding a new token tracking hook that captures provider-specific token counts from API responses.
key_requirements: Add token usage tracking for all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Integrate with existing instrumentation system to intercept method calls
Extract token counts from provider API response metadata/headers
Store token data in answer metadata for visibility
constraints: Must leverage existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook patterns
Token extraction must work across all LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider)
Implementation should not require invasive changes to existing provider interfaces
stakeholders: LLM users who need to monitor query costs
Application developers responsible for cost optimization
System administrators requiring visibility into token usage patterns

in_scope: Token usage tracking for LLM queries
Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama providers
Exposure of token data in answer metadata or logs
out_of_scope: Implementation of new LLM provider integrations beyond OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama
Modification of core LLM provider interfaces or logic
Development of cost billing or payment systems based on token usage

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/core/token_tracker.py — Core token tracking logic and hook implementation

assumptions.assumption: Token counts are available via API responses from providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) - typically in response headers or metadata
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, token tracking implementation will fail to capture usage data, leading to incomplete cost monitoring capabilities

assumptions.assumption: The existing instrumentation system provides adequate mechanism for hook integration
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, the implementation approach may not work with current architecture, requiring significant redesign of the tracking system

assumptions.assumption: Answer structure can accommodate new 'tokens_used' field in metadata
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, token data cannot be surfaced to users for cost monitoring and optimization

approaches.name: Extend Provider Protocols with Structured Responses
approaches.description: Introduce new protocols or modify existing ones to return structured data including token usage metadata. Update provider implementations like OpenAIChat to return enhanced types that include usage information alongside content.
pros: Enables full backward compatibility for current consumers while adding new capability
Leverages existing protocol-based abstraction patterns in the codebase
cons: Requires changes across multiple provider implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Introduces potential breaking changes if not versioned carefully
best_for: Systems requiring detailed token tracking and cost monitoring

approaches.name: Instrumentation Hook with Post-Processing Parsing
approaches.description: Use existing instrumentation system to wrap provider calls, parsing raw API responses for usage metadata after the fact. Create a TokenAccumulationHook that intercepts results from providers supporting token data.
pros: Minimal changes required since hooks already exist in InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook
Does not modify core protocol contracts or provider logic
cons: Only works if providers return usage metadata directly (which they don't currently)
Cannot retroactively extract token data from string-based responses like OpenAIChat.chat()
best_for: Quick prototype or temporary solution before full protocol extension

architecture.recommended: Extend Provider Protocols with Structured Responses
architecture.reasoning: This approach directly addresses the root cause identified in the analysis: all current provider protocols return basic types (str, list[float]) without token metadata. The recommended approach extends these contracts to support structured responses that include usage data while maintaining backward compatibility where possible. This enables true end-to-end tracking and aggregation of token costs across multiple LLM calls within the engine. The instrumentation hook approach is not viable because current providers like OpenAIChat.chat() return only strings without embedded usage metadata, making it impossible for hooks to extract such information from raw responses.

key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_enhanced_funct: Extending provider protocols to include token usage metadata breaks existing implementations that expect simple return types like str. This requires either introducing new protocols or deprecating old ones, which impacts backward compatibility.
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_system_modification_vs_n: Modifying the existing instrumentation system to handle structured responses with usage data versus creating a separate TokenAccumulationHook introduces complexity in maintaining consistency and avoiding duplication of logic across systems.

technology_considerations: Python TypedDict for defining structured return types that include token usage metadata
Protocol-based abstraction to support both legacy string-returning providers and new enhanced ones
InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook system for integrating token tracking into existing instrumentation workflows
Dataclass decorator on Answer class to enable easy extension with token_usage field
OpenAI SDK integration pattern to extract usage data from API responses
architecture.scope_statement: Extend core provider protocols to support structured returns with token usage data and update Answer class to store accumulated usage metrics across multiple LLM invocations.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend Provider Protocols with Token Usage Support
adrs.context: The current codebase lacks token usage tracking infrastructure to monitor how many LLM tokens each query costs. The core issue stems from missing protocol support in provider interfaces that return basic types without token metadata.
adrs.decision: Introduce new protocols or extend existing ones (ChatProviderWithUsage) to allow structured responses including token usage data, while maintaining backward compatibility through optional adoption.
adrs.rationale: Current ChatProvider.chat() returns only str but proposed solution assumes structured responses with embedded usage metadata. The instrumentation system operates on raw function calls without access to usage data unless providers are modified to include it in their return types. This change enables tracking at provider level and aggregation in engine components without breaking existing implementations.
consequences: Providers must be updated to support new return types that include usage metadata
Engine components will need logic to accumulate token usage across multiple calls
Answer class requires modification to store accumulated token usage from multiple LLM invocations
API response models may need extension to expose token_usage information
alternatives_considered: Keep existing protocols unchanged and add token tracking separately via wrapper functions - rejected because it breaks the abstraction layer and creates maintenance overhead

Modify all provider interfaces directly to return structured data including usage - rejected because this would break backward compatibility with existing implementations that depend on string returns

adrs.title: ADR: Update Answer Class to Include Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The Answer object currently has no mechanism to store accumulated token usage from multiple LLM calls, making it impossible to track query costs.
adrs.decision: Add a token_usage field to the Answer dataclass with optional dictionary type to support token accumulation across provider invocations.
adrs.rationale: Current Answer class uses @dataclass but omits any field for tracking token usage. This simple addition allows capturing accumulated token counts from engine-level processing without requiring changes to other core components that depend on Answer objects.
consequences: Answer creation will need to handle optional token_usage parameter
Engine integration must populate this field during query processing
API responses can now include usage data if needed for observability
alternatives_considered: Modify Answer class to always require token_usage - rejected because it would break existing code that creates Answers without tracking context
Store token usage in a separate global context or thread-local storage - rejected because this approach is harder to reason about and maintain consistency across async operations

adrs.title: ADR: Implement TokenAccumulationHook for Instrumentation System Integration
adrs.context: The instrumentation system exists but doesn't currently capture token usage data from provider calls. The existing BenchmarkHook.on_call_end method accepts Any result type, which can support structured responses with usage metadata.
adrs.decision: Create a TokenAccumulationHook that works with the existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook framework to aggregate token usage across multiple provider invocations during query processing.
adrs.rationale: The instrumentation system already supports generic hooks through BenchmarkHook protocol. By implementing this hook, we can integrate token tracking into the existing monitoring infrastructure without major architectural changes. It leverages current patterns from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for consistent behavior.
consequences: New hook class needs to be registered conditionally via configuration or instrumentation system
InstrumentedProxy will need to support passing usage data through method calls
Query processing pipeline must ensure hooks are properly attached during execution
alternatives_considered: Build custom tracking mechanism outside of instrumentation system - rejected because it duplicates functionality and reduces observability consistency
Implement token counting at the API layer only - rejected because this loses granular detail about which providers were called and their individual costs

artifacts.purpose: Defines the base provider protocols and introduces a new protocol with token usage support

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

dataclass
from typing import Optional, Dict

from fitz_ai.core.answer_mode import AnswerMode
from fitz_ai.core.provenance import Provenance

@dataclass
class Answer:
    content: str
    provenance: Provenance  
    token_usage: Optional[Dict[str, int]] = None
artifacts.purpose: Updates the Answer class to include a field for tracking accumulated token usage
Modifies the FitzKragEngine to accumulate and store token usage from LLM calls

artifacts.content:
# ... existing imports ...
from pydantic import BaseModel
# ... rest of file imports ...

from fitz_ai.core.answer import Answer


class QueryResponse(BaseModel):
    answer: Answer
    token_usage: Optional[dict] = None  # Add if not present

async def query(request: QueryRequest) -> QueryResponse:
    # ... existing implementation ...
    
    response = QueryResponse(
        answer=answer,
        token_usage=answer.token_usage
    )
    return response

architecture.recommended: Extend Provider Protocols with Structured Responses
architecture.reasoning: This approach directly addresses the root cause identified in the analysis: all current provider protocols return basic types (str, list[float]) without token metadata. The recommended approach extends these contracts to support structured responses that include usage data while maintaining backward compatibility where possible. This enables true end-to-end tracking and aggregation of token costs across multiple LLM calls within the engine. The instrumentation hook approach is not viable because current providers like OpenAIChat.chat() return only strings without embedded usage metadata, making it impossible for hooks to extract such information from raw responses.

key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_enhanced_funct: Extending provider protocols to include token usage metadata breaks existing implementations that expect simple return types like str. This requires either introducing new protocols or deprecating old ones, which impacts backward compatibility.

technology_considerations: Python TypedDict for defining structured return types that include token usage metadata

InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook system for integrating token tracking into existing instrumentation workflows
Dataclass decorator on Answer class to enable easy extension with token_usage field

architecture.scope_statement: Extend core provider protocols to support structured returns with token usage data and update Answer class to store accumulated usage metrics across multiple LLM invocations.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend Provider Protocols with Token Usage Support
adrs.context: The current codebase lacks token usage tracking infrastructure to monitor how many LLM tokens each query costs. The core issue stems from missing protocol support in provider interfaces that return basic types without token metadata.
adrs.decision: Introduce new protocols or extend existing ones (ChatProviderWithUsage) to allow structured responses including token usage data, while maintaining backward compatibility through optional adoption.
adrs.rationale: Current ChatProvider.chat() returns only str but proposed solution assumes structured responses with embedded usage metadata. The instrumentation system operates on raw function calls without access to usage data unless providers are modified to include it in their return types. This change enables tracking at provider level and aggregation in engine components without breaking existing implementations.
consequences: Providers must be updated to support new return types that include usage metadata
Engine components will need logic to accumulate token usage across multiple calls
Answer class requires modification to store accumulated token usage from multiple LLM invocations
API response models may need extension to expose token_usage information
alternatives_considered: Keep existing protocols unchanged and add token tracking separately via wrapper functions - rejected because it breaks the abstraction layer and creates maintenance overhead

Modify all provider interfaces directly to return structured data including usage - rejected because this would break backward compatibility with existing implementations that depend on string returns

adrs.title: ADR: Update Answer Class to Include Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The Answer object currently has no mechanism to store accumulated token usage from multiple LLM calls, making it impossible to track query costs.
adrs.decision: Add a token_usage field to the Answer dataclass with optional dictionary type to support token accumulation across provider invocations.
adrs.rationale: Current Answer class uses @dataclass but omits any field for tracking token usage. This simple addition allows capturing accumulated token counts from engine-level processing without requiring changes to other core components that depend on Answer objects.
consequences: Answer creation will need to handle optional token_usage parameter
Engine integration must populate this field during query processing
API responses can now include usage data if needed for observability
alternatives_considered: Modify Answer class to always require token_usage - rejected because it would break existing code that creates Answers without tracking context
Store token usage in a separate global context or thread-local storage - rejected because this approach is harder to reason about and maintain consistency across async operations

adrs.title: ADR: Implement TokenAccumulationHook for Instrumentation System Integration
adrs.context: The instrumentation system exists but doesn't currently capture token usage data from provider calls. The existing BenchmarkHook.on_call_end method accepts Any result type, which can support structured responses with usage metadata.
adrs.decision: Create a TokenAccumulationHook that works with the existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook framework to aggregate token usage across multiple provider invocations during query processing.
adrs.rationale: The instrumentation system already supports generic hooks through BenchmarkHook protocol. By implementing this hook, we can integrate token tracking into the existing monitoring infrastructure without major architectural changes. It leverages current patterns from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for consistent behavior.
consequences: New hook class needs to be registered conditionally via configuration or instrumentation system
InstrumentedProxy will need to support passing usage data through method calls
Query processing pipeline must ensure hooks are properly attached during execution
alternatives_considered: Build custom tracking mechanism outside of instrumentation system - rejected because it duplicates functionality and reduces observability consistency
Implement token counting at the API layer only - rejected because this loses granular detail about which providers were called and their individual costs

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Extend Provider Protocols
phases.objective: Modify base provider protocols to include token usage metadata in responses
deliverables: Updated ChatProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated EmbeddingProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated RerankProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated VisionProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Breaking changes to existing provider implementations
Inconsistent token usage reporting across different providers

phases.name: Implement Token Usage in Providers
phases.objective: Update concrete provider implementations to return structured token usage data
key_risks: Provider-specific API differences affecting uniform token usage reporting
Performance overhead from additional metadata processing

phases.name: Integrate Token Usage into Answer Dataclass
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking field to the Answer dataclass for accumulating metadata
deliverables: Updated Answer dataclass with token_usage field
Integration of token usage from provider responses during query processing
key_risks: Potential performance impact when accumulating large amounts of usage data
Inconsistent handling of token usage across different engine components

phases.name: Add Instrumentation Support for Tracking
phases.objective: Enhance instrumentation system to capture and report token usage metrics from provider calls
deliverables: Instrumented proxy wrapper enhancements for tracking LLM calls with token data
New hooks in instrumentation system for capturing token usage
key_risks: Potential race conditions or performance bottlenecks in concurrent token tracking
Complexity of maintaining compatibility with existing hooks

phases.name: Expose Token Usage in SDK and API
phases.objective: Make token usage data accessible through SDK interface and API responses for end users
deliverables: Updated fitz.ask() to include token usage in returned answers
Updated query API endpoint to return token usage metadata
Documentation updates covering new token usage tracking features
key_risks: API backward compatibility issues with existing consumers of the SDK or API
Potential confusion for users regarding how to interpret different types of token usage

risks.description: Provider protocols currently return basic types (str, list[float], etc.) which do not include token usage metadata, requiring protocol modifications to support tracking.
risks.mitigation: Extend base provider protocols in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py with structured return types that include TokenUsage metadata. Update all concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to populate this data.
risks.contingency: If protocol changes are not feasible immediately, implement a wrapper layer that parses raw responses for token usage where supported (e.g., OpenAI's response headers), but acknowledge limited coverage across providers.
risks.verification: Verify that new TokenUsage fields are populated in all provider implementations and included in Answer objects

risks.description: Instrumentation system cannot extract token usage from raw return values without modifying providers to include structured metadata.
risks.mitigation: Modify existing provider implementations and base protocols to return structured data including token usage instead of just basic types like str or list[float].
risks.contingency: Implement a fallback mechanism that extracts tokens from response headers where available (e.g., OpenAI's x-request-id, x-ratelimit-remaining), but note this is provider-specific and incomplete.
risks.verification: Confirm that Answer objects contain token usage data by examining test cases or running queries with different providers

risks.description: Answer dataclass does not currently track token usage, requiring modification to accumulate metadata from LLM calls.
risks.mitigation: Add a new field to Answer class in fitz_ai/core/answer.py to store total token usage information (input_tokens, output_tokens) for tracking query cost.
risks.contingency: If modifying Answer is not immediately possible, create an auxiliary metadata mapping or extend Answer with a separate token_usage attribute that gets populated during query processing.
risks.verification: Ensure all calls to answer() method result in populated token usage data within Answer objects

recommended_contingencies: Implement a wrapper layer for providers that parse response headers for token usage where supported (OpenAI), while planning full protocol modifications as primary approach
Create an auxiliary metadata collection mechanism to track token usage even before Answer class is modified
Add instrumentation hooks specifically designed to capture and aggregate token usage metrics during LLM calls

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Extend Provider Protocols
phases.objective: Modify base provider protocols to include token usage metadata in responses
deliverables: Updated ChatProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated EmbeddingProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated RerankProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
Updated VisionProvider protocol with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Breaking changes to existing provider implementations
Inconsistent token usage reporting across different providers

phases.name: Implement Token Usage in Providers
phases.objective: Update concrete provider implementations to return structured token usage data
key_risks: Provider-specific API differences affecting uniform token usage reporting
Performance overhead from additional metadata processing

phases.name: Integrate Token Usage into Answer Dataclass
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking field to the Answer dataclass for accumulating metadata
deliverables: Updated Answer dataclass with token_usage field
Integration of token usage from provider responses during query processing
key_risks: Potential performance impact when accumulating large amounts of usage data
Inconsistent handling of token usage across different engine components

phases.name: Add Instrumentation Support for Tracking
phases.objective: Enhance instrumentation system to capture and report token usage metrics from provider calls
deliverables: Instrumented proxy wrapper enhancements for tracking LLM calls with token data
New hooks in instrumentation system for capturing token usage
key_risks: Potential race conditions or performance bottlenecks in concurrent token tracking
Complexity of maintaining compatibility with existing hooks

phases.name: Expose Token Usage in SDK and API
phases.objective: Make token usage data accessible through SDK interface and API responses for end users
deliverables: Updated fitz.ask() to include token usage in returned answers
Updated query API endpoint to return token usage metadata
Documentation updates covering new token usage tracking features
key_risks: API backward compatibility issues with existing consumers of the SDK or API
Potential confusion for users regarding how to interpret different types of token usage

risks.description: Provider protocols currently return basic types (str, list[float], etc.) which do not include token usage metadata, requiring protocol modifications to support tracking.
risks.mitigation: Extend base provider protocols in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py with structured return types that include TokenUsage metadata. Update all concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to populate this data.
risks.contingency: If protocol changes are not feasible immediately, implement a wrapper layer that parses raw responses for token usage where supported (e.g., OpenAI's response headers), but acknowledge limited coverage across providers.
risks.verification: Verify that new TokenUsage fields are populated in all provider implementations and included in Answer objects

risks.description: Instrumentation system cannot extract token usage from raw return values without modifying providers to include structured metadata.
risks.mitigation: Modify existing provider implementations and base protocols to return structured data including token usage instead of just basic types like str or list[float].
risks.contingency: Implement a fallback mechanism that extracts tokens from response headers where available (e.g., OpenAI's x-request-id, x-ratelimit-remaining), but note this is provider-specific and incomplete.
risks.verification: Confirm that Answer objects contain token usage data by examining test cases or running queries with different providers

risks.description: Answer dataclass does not currently track token usage, requiring modification to accumulate metadata from LLM calls.
risks.mitigation: Add a new field to Answer class in fitz_ai/core/answer.py to store total token usage information (input_tokens, output_tokens) for tracking query cost.
risks.contingency: If modifying Answer is not immediately possible, create an auxiliary metadata mapping or extend Answer with a separate token_usage attribute that gets populated during query processing.
risks.verification: Ensure all calls to answer() method result in populated token usage data within Answer objects

recommended_contingencies: Implement a wrapper layer for providers that parse response headers for token usage where supported (OpenAI), while planning full protocol modifications as primary approach
Create an auxiliary metadata collection mechanism to track token usage even before Answer class is modified
Add instrumentation hooks specifically designed to capture and aggregate token usage metrics during LLM calls
